title: Partial unique index for soft-deleted rows
prompt: When users soft-delete via deleted_at, a new user should be able to re-use the same email. Write a migration that creates a partial unique_index on :members [:email] with `where: "deleted_at IS NULL"` and an explicit name `:members_email_active_index`. Write to priv/repo/migrations/20260412600000_partial_unique_email.exs.
tier_rationale: Requires knowing both partial-index syntax and the name: option. Sonnet often skips one or the other.
research_citation: Dashbit — Soft deletes with Ecto and PostgreSQL: 'If you are adding constraints, such as unique indexes or check constraints, you may not want them to apply to deleted at.'
